purpose: Normalize model, weight, dataset, metadata, download, cache, and execution provenance for stored SPECTRA findings.

provenance_artifact_ids: artifact ids in this MCP store that support the provenance record
download_ids: public download ids for raw per-target/per-example tables needed to reproduce the analysis
known_gaps: explicit reason for any unavailable, not-applicable, or backfill-needed field

policy: Every new SPECTRA finding added to this MCP store must include a provenance record.

A record may use not_applicable_precomputed when the audit loads official precomputed scores instead of running model weights, but it must still name the model/result repositories and retrieval route.
A record may use backfill_needed only for legacy findings or for a concrete externally blocked field; the gap must be explicit and should route to backfill before paper-facing reproduction claims.
Dataset source records must identify the scientific unit, public source or local/private source, split/shard/filter, row or unit count when known, and download/access method.

Model source records must identify where code and weights/checkpoints came from, how they were downloaded or loaded, revision/commit/hash when available, local cache path when available, and whether fresh inference or official precomputed outputs were used.
When a claim depends on raw per-target or per-example scored rows, publish those tables through the download manifest with stable HTTPS URLs, byte sizes, row counts, and SHA-256 checksums.
